content: 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory systems can be rearchitected to prioritize storing only the high-surprise, low-prediction-confidence transitions that curiosity-driven RL methods like ICM and RND explicitly flag, rather than uniformly caching all interaction trajectories. This targeted memory expansion not only reduces memory bloat for long-horizon agents but also creates a self-reinforcing cycle where curiosity-driven exploration is amplified by access to past high-information surprising experiences that would otherwise be forgotten.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
